study_title: RNA seq reads of ant Temnothorax nylanderi and cestode parasite Anomotaenia brevis from samples of colonies with differring parasite prevalence
exp_title: RNA-seq of Tenmnothorax nylanderi: fat body
design: We isolated the fat body from each worker by removing the gut and trachea from the first gaster segment, leaving only the fat cells, which were placed in a vial containing 50 l Trizol. Samples were frozen on dry ice and subsequently transferred to -80C.  RNA was extracted using the Qiagen RNeasy extraction kit. We sent the samples to Novogene (Cambridge, UK) for mRNA library preparation using a NEBNext Ultra RNA Library Prep Kit. The libraries were sequenced on an Illumina Novaseq 6000 in paired-end 150 bp mode, yielding a minimum of 9 Gb per sample.

design: We isolated the fat body from each worker by removing the gut and trachea from the first gaster segment, leaving only the fat cells, which were placed in a vial containing 50 l Trizol. Samples were frozen on dry ice and subsequently transferred to -80C.  RNA was extracted using the Qiagen RNeasy extraction kit. We sent the samples to Novogene (Cambridge, UK) for mRNA library preparation using a NEBNext Ultra RNA Library Prep Kit. The libraries were sequenced on an Illumina Novaseq 6000 in paired-end 150 bp mode, yielding a minimum of 9 Gb per sample.

study_title: The influence of parasite load on transcriptional activity and morphology of a cestode and its ant intermediate host
exp_title: RNA seq of Temnothorax nylanderi: fat body
design: Sequencing was conducted by Novogene (Cambridge, UK). They enriched the mRNA using oligo(dT) beads, then fragmented randomly by adding fragmentation buffer. The cDNA was synthesized by using mRNA template and random hexamers primer, after which a custom second-strand synthesis buffer (Illumina), dNTPs, RNase H and DNA polymerase I were added to initiate the second-strand synthesis. After a series of terminal repair, A ligation and sequencing adaptor ligation, the cDNA libraries were completed through size selection and PCR enrichment. Illumina sequencing was conducted after pooling according to the libraries effective concentration and expected data volume (Novogene Co., Ltd). We sequenced a minimum of 9Gb per sample, resulting in around 30 million paired-end 150bp reads.
sample_description: lowly infected sample from colony 106

exp_title: RNA seq of Temnothorax nylanderi: fat body
design: Sequencing was conducted by Novogene (Cambridge, UK). They enriched the mRNA using oligo(dT) beads, then fragmented randomly by adding fragmentation buffer. The cDNA was synthesized by using mRNA template and random hexamers primer, after which a custom second-strand synthesis buffer (Illumina), dNTPs, RNase H and DNA polymerase I were added to initiate the second-strand synthesis. After a series of terminal repair, A ligation and sequencing adaptor ligation, the cDNA libraries were completed through size selection and PCR enrichment. Illumina sequencing was conducted after pooling according to the libraries effective concentration and expected data volume (Novogene Co., Ltd). We sequenced a minimum of 9Gb per sample, resulting in around 30 million paired-end 150bp reads.

study_title: Transcriptomics of queen-worker pairs in slavemaking ants and its hosts
exp_title: RNA-Seq of Temnothorax nylanderi Worker
design: RNA extraction of all samples was conducted in September 2017 using the Nucleo-Spin Mini kit (Macherey-Nagel). For each species, we analyzed a pool of 6 workers and another pool of three queens. We extracted RNA from whole bodies, as we had no prior assumptions as to which tissue differed most strongly in gene expression between our groups.  Samples were shipped to StarSEQ (Mainz) for library preparation and 100bp paired-end sequencing on an Illumina HiSeq. In total, we obtained 15 million reads on average per sample.

study_title: Gene expression analyses of an ant host and cestode parasite shed light on parasite-induced changes and parasite metabolism
exp_title: RNA-Seq of Temnothorax nylanderi: abdomen of healthy queen from healthy nest rep2
design: Ant colonies were collected in October 2017 and 2018 from the Lenneberg forest near Mainz, Germany. Ants were transported to the Johannes Gutenberg University Mainz, relocated to slide nests composed of a Plexiglas cavity sandwiched between two microscope slides, and placed in three-chambered nesting boxes. Until sampling, ants were kept at 22C and fed with honey twice weekly but no crickets to prevent contamination of the gut content. Water was provided ad libitum. In November 2017, we selected four infected colonies and transferred four infected and four non-infected workers from the brood-pile per colony into small Petri dishes. Infected workers are mostly found directly on the brood although they show less brood care activity (Scharf et al., 2012). Infection status was preliminarily assessed by the color of the cuticle since infected workers have a yellow cuticle compared to the typical brown cuticle of their uninfected nestmates (Trabalon et al., 2000). However, note that in infected colonies, sometimes infected workers occur with a normal brown cuticle, and without dissection, infection status cannot be determined with certainty (Scharf et al., 2012). We, therefore, confirmed infection status later by mapping the reads of each sample against the cestode transcriptome. The gaster of the ants was detached with a razor blade directly in front of the postpetiole and four abdomens per colony were pooled into 100 l of Trizol to reduce variance between samples. Abdomens were directly crushed in Trizol, which was kept on ice and protected from direct light. Samples were stored under -80C until RNA extraction, which was performed using the RNAeasy mini extraction kit (Qiagen) following the standard protocol and sent to the Institute of Molecular Biology Mainz (IMB) for sequencing on an Illumina NextSeq500. The entire procedure was repeated exactly one year later, in 2018, only this time we selected four uninfected colonies and obtained one pool of four abdomens of again inside workers directly on the brood and one sample of the abdomen of the queen per colony. Thus, the location inside the nest was the same for all our four groups.
